42.kj: [phrase] afflicted, age, (Ahoh-) (Ammon-) (Hachmon-) (Lev-) ite, (anoint-) ed one, appointed to, ([phrase]) arrow, (Assyr-) (Babylon-) (Egypt-) (Grec-) ian, one born, bough, branch

42.gl: the head (as most easily shaken), whether literal or figurative (in many applications, of place, time, rank, itc.)
42.kj: band, beginning, captain, chapiter, chief(-est place, man, things), company, end, [idiom] every (man), excellent, first, forefront, (be-)head, height, (on) high(-est part, (priest)
42.dv: from an unused root apparently meaning to shake;

42.gl: a Reubenite or descendant of Reuben

43.gl: a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense (of literal and figurative relationship, including grandson, subject, nation, quality or condition, etc., (like father or brother), etc.)
43.kj: [phrase] afflicted, age, (Ahoh-) (Ammon-) (Hachmon-) (Lev-) ite, (anoint-) ed one, appointed to, ([phrase]) arrow, (Assyr-) (Babylon-) (Egypt-) (Grec-) ian, one born, bough, branch

43.gl: Maakah (or Maakath), the name of a place in Syria, also of a Mesopotamian, of three Israelites, and of four Israelitesses and one Syrian woman
43.kj: Maachah, Maachathites. See also H1038 (בֵּית מַעֲכָה).

43.gl: a Mithnite, or inhabitant of Methen
43.dv: probably patrial from an unused noun meaning slenderness;

43.gl: Chanan, the name of seven Israelites

44.gl: a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense (of literal and figurative relationship, including grandson, subject, nation, quality or condition, etc., (like father or brother), etc.)
44.kj: [phrase] afflicted, age, (Ahoh-) (Ammon-) (Hachmon-) (Lev-) ite, (anoint-) ed one, appointed to, ([phrase]) arrow, (Assyr-) (Babylon-) (Egypt-) (Grec-) ian, one born, bough, branch

44.gl: Jeiel, the name of six Israelites
44.dv: from H3261 (יָעָה) and H410 (אֵל); carried away of God;

44.gl: an Ashterathite or inhabitant of Ashtaroth
Chotham, the name of two Israelites
an Aroerite or inhabitant of Aroer

45.gl: a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense (of literal and figurative relationship, including grandson, subject, nation, quality or condition, etc., (like father or brother), etc.)
45.kj: [phrase] afflicted, age, (Ahoh-) (Ammon-) (Hachmon-) (Lev-) ite, (anoint-) ed one, appointed to, ([phrase]) arrow, (Assyr-) (Babylon-) (Egypt-) (Grec-) ian, one born, bough, branch

45.gl: a brother (used in the widest sense of literal relationship and metaphorical affinity or resemblance (like father))
45.kj: another, brother(-ly); kindred, like, other. Compare also the proper names beginning with 'Ah-' or 'Ahi-'.

45.gl: a Titsite or descendant or inhabitant of an unknown Tits
45.dv: patrial or patronymically from an unused noun of uncertain meaning;

45.gl: Jocha, the name of two Israelites
45.dv: probably from H3068 (יְהֹוָה) and a variation of H2421 (חָיָה); Jehovah-revived;

45.gl: Shimri, the name of four Israelites
45.dv: from H8105 (שֶׁמֶר) in its original sense; watchful;

45.gl: Jediael, the name of three Israelites
45.dv: from H3045 (יָדַע) and H410 (אֵל); knowing God;

46.gl: a son (as a builder of the family name), in the widest sense (of literal and figurative relationship, including grandson, subject, nation, quality or condition, etc., (like father or brother), etc.)
46.kj: [phrase] afflicted, age, (Ahoh-) (Ammon-) (Hachmon-) (Lev-) ite, (anoint-) ed one, appointed to, ([phrase]) arrow, (Assyr-) (Babylon-) (Egypt-) (Grec-) ian, one born, bough, branch

46.gl: a Moabite or Moabitess, i.e. a descendant from Moab
46.dv: feminine מוֹאָבִיָּה; or מוֹאָבִיתxlit Môwâbîyth corrected to Môwʼâbîyth; patronymical from H4124 (מוֹאָב);

46.gl: a Machavite or inhabitant of some place named Machaveh
46.dv: apparently a patrial, but from an unknown place (in the plural only for a singular);

46.gl: Eliel, the name of nine Israelites
46.dv: from H410 (אֵל) repeated; God of (his) God;
from H410 (אֵל) and H5276 (נָעֵם); God (is his) delight;

47.dv: apparently from H4672 (מָצָא) and H3050 (יָהּ); found of Jah;
from H6213 (עָשָׂה) and H410 (אֵל); made of God;

47.gl: Eliel, the name of nine Israelites
47.dv: from H410 (אֵל) repeated; God of (his) God;

47.gl: Obed, the name of five Israelites
